mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals expanding aggregate wage income and consumer demand, which drives upward revisions to corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 144448K shows steady expansion through mid-2016
failure_condition: If month-over-month increases in PAYEMS coincide with negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of observations over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% sustains suppressed discount rates and elevated risk asset valuations; maintained accommodation generates positive S&P 500 forward returns as investors are pushed out the risk curve.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in over 50% of months when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% during any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales (RSAFS) and decreases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirm a demand-pull expansion where consumption strength coincides with labor market tightening, jointly amplifying corporate pricing power and generating positive S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 457727 and UNRATE at 4.9% as of mid-2016
failure_condition: If the interaction term of RSAFS growth and UNRATE decline fails to produce a Rank IC greater than 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or underperforms both single-proxy models.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions when unemployment is below 5.0%, month-over-month increases in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signal overheating that forces the Federal Reserve to tighten policy faster than discounted, raising the equity risk premium and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 5.0% and month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 239.927 and UNRATE at 4.9% as of mid-2016
failure_condition: If in months where UNRATE < 5.0% and CPIAUCSL rose, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur more than 50% of the time over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal weakening manufacturing activity and capacity utilization, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 returns as equity risk premia expand.
failure_condition: If month-over-month declines in INDPRO are followed by positive S&P 500 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts (HOUST) lead construction and durable goods demand with a short lag; positive surprises signal broadening cyclical momentum that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If monthly increases in HOUST coincide with negative S&P 500 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of observations over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points over three months signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes consumer spending contractions, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp in 3 months are followed by positive S&P 500 60-day returns in more than 50% of cases over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter annualized growth in real GDP (GDPC1) above 2.0% signals demand outperformance relative to potential, lifting corporate profit expectations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.0% are followed by negative S&P 500 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 3-year window.
